diffs.diff:
--- a/patch_fix.py
+++ b/patch_fix.py
@@ -1,11 +1,51 @@
 from pathlib import Path

 p = Path('predict.py')

 s = p.read_text()

-old = 'ax, ay = agent_pos(env)'

-new = 'ax, ay = tuple(int(v) for v in env.agent_pos)'

+old = '''            if getattr(env, "carrying", None) is not None and target_type != "key":

+                # If we are adjacent to the final pickup target while holding a key,

+                # make room in inventory first.  Dropping is only possible into the

+                # front cell, so after rotating toward an empty neighbor we must drop

+                # on the next step instead of rotating back toward the target.

+                ax, ay = tuple(int(v) for v in env.agent_pos)

+                if abs(ax - p[0]) + abs(ay - p[1]) == 1:

+                    fp = front_pos(env)

+                    if fp != p and get_cell(env, fp) is None:

+                        return 4

+                    for ep in neighbors4((ax, ay)):

+                        if ep != p and get_cell(env, ep) is None:

+                            da = action_to_face(env, ep)

+                            if da is not None:

+                                return da

+                    return random_safe_action(env)

+'''

+new = '''            if getattr(env, "carrying", None) is not None and target_type != "key":

+                # If we are adjacent to the final pickup target while holding a key,

+                # make room in inventory first.  Never return pickup in this state:

+                # pickup is a no-op while carrying, causing KeyCorridor/UnlockPickup

+                # to stall at the box/ball.  Prefer dropping into an empty/non-target

+                # front cell; if facing the target, rotate away until a drop is legal.

+                ax, ay = tuple(int(v) for v in env.agent_pos)

+                pp = (int(p[0]), int(p[1]))

+                if abs(ax - pp[0]) + abs(ay - pp[1]) == 1:

+                    fp = front_pos(env)

+                    if fp != pp:

+                        fc = get_cell(env, fp)

+                        if fc is None:

+                            return 4

+                        # If not facing the target, try dropping anyway; Minigrid will

+                        # ignore occupied cells, but this avoids falling through to pickup.

+                        return 4

+                    # Facing target: turn toward any other empty neighbor; if none is

+                    # known empty, simply turn left to break the pickup loop.

+                    for ep in neighbors4((ax, ay)):

+                        if ep != pp and get_cell(env, ep) is None:

+                            da = action_to_face(env, ep)

+                            if da is not None:

+                                return da

+                    return 0

+'''

 if old not in s:

-    print('target string not found; no replacement made')

+    print('old block not found')

 else:

-    s = s.replace(old, new)

-    p.write_text(s)

-    print('patched undefined agent_pos call')

+    p.write_text(s.replace(old, new))

+    print('patched robust no-pickup while carrying near final target')

--- a/predict.py
+++ b/predict.py
@@ -317,20 +317,29 @@
             # before the final pickup.  Drop into an empty neighboring cell first.

             if getattr(env, "carrying", None) is not None and target_type != "key":

                 # If we are adjacent to the final pickup target while holding a key,

-                # make room in inventory first.  Dropping is only possible into the

-                # front cell, so after rotating toward an empty neighbor we must drop

-                # on the next step instead of rotating back toward the target.

+                # make room in inventory first.  Never return pickup in this state:

+                # pickup is a no-op while carrying, causing KeyCorridor/UnlockPickup

+                # to stall at the box/ball.  Prefer dropping into an empty/non-target

+                # front cell; if facing the target, rotate away until a drop is legal.

                 ax, ay = tuple(int(v) for v in env.agent_pos)

-                if abs(ax - p[0]) + abs(ay - p[1]) == 1:

+                pp = (int(p[0]), int(p[1]))

+                if abs(ax - pp[0]) + abs(ay - pp[1]) == 1:

                     fp = front_pos(env)

-                    if fp != p and get_cell(env, fp) is None:

+                    if fp != pp:

+                        fc = get_cell(env, fp)

+                        if fc is None:

+                            return 4

+                        # If not facing the target, try dropping anyway; Minigrid will

+                        # ignore occupied cells, but this avoids falling through to pickup.

                         return 4

+                    # Facing target: turn toward any other empty neighbor; if none is

+                    # known empty, simply turn left to break the pickup loop.

                     for ep in neighbors4((ax, ay)):

-                        if ep != p and get_cell(env, ep) is None:

+                        if ep != pp and get_cell(env, ep) is None:

                             da = action_to_face(env, ep)

                             if da is not None:

                                 return da

-                    return random_safe_action(env)

+                    return 0

             if front_pos(env) == p:

                 return 3

             a = action_to_face(env, p)
